summary: Good tape capture of a mixed Worcester 1984 show featuring strong Bird Song, Lazy Lightnin > Supplication and Truckin', but overall an uneven performance.
review: The recording was made with high‑end cassette gear right beside the soundboard, yielding a clear and well‑preserved tape despite being an audience source. Musically the concert is a mixed bag – the first set feels uninspired and the second set has standout moments like a soaring Bird Song, a tight Lazy Lightnin > Supplication sequence and a solid Truckin', while the rendition of I Just Want to Make Love to You falls flat.

ai_rating.rationale: Reviews average ~3‑star with both positive (notable jams) and negative (uneven first set, weak I Just Want…) comments; recording quality is praised while performance is mixed.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Recorded with Mike Rice's D‑5/D‑6 decks and Nakamichi CM‑100s using CP‑4 shotgun caps; tape sound described as great and crisp, no major dropouts noted.

poor_songs: I Just Want to Make Love to You
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is described as uninspired and never really blazes, while the second set opens unusually with Terrapin and delivers several strong jams (Bird Song, Lazy Lightnin > Supplication, Samson > Make Love) giving the show an uneven but occasionally spectacular flow.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for ripping during the Samson > Make Love jam, though noted some tension over song selection with Brent.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent referenced regarding setlist decisions; no specific keyboard performance comments.
band_member_comments.Drums: Drummers receive a solid groove after the space segment and subsequent jam; no detailed critique.
